## Review: agent-a365bcf2 iter 1

### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES

### AC Grades

| # | Item | Grade | Evidence |
|---|------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Preset IDs exposed: cheap, default, smart, minimal, benchmark. Old IDs retained as deprecated aliases with stderr warning | APPROVE | internal/prompt/presets.go:166-167 — deprecated aliases resolve with warning |
| 2 | --preset flag help string lists new names first; old names deprecated | APPROVE | cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:65 — help text updated with new names and deprecation note |
| 3 | README.md and SD-003 updated to use new names | BLOCK   | — neither file appears in diff |
| 4 | Unit test asserts --preset codex resolves and emits warning | APPROVE | internal/prompt/presets_test.go:54-55 — `TestResolvePresetName` tests codex→cheap |
| 5 | Unit test asserts new names resolve to correct preset bodies | APPROVE | internal/prompt/presets_test.go:34-56 — explicit tests for default, smart, cheap |
| 6 | CHANGELOG.md entry documenting rename and deprecation timeline | APPROVE | CHANGELOG.md:8-13 — includes migration note and future removal mention |
| 7 | Follow-up bead filed for hard removal in future release | BLOCK   | — no evidence of bead filing in diff |

### Summary

The core preset renaming and deprecation infrastructure is correctly implemented. However, two acceptance criteria are not verifiable from the diff: documentation updates (AC3) and a follow-up bead for future alias removal (AC7).

### Findings

- **AC3 (BLOCK):** `README.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-003-system-prompts.md` are not modified in the diff. These files must be updated to use new preset names per acceptance criteria #3. Search for any remaining references to `agent`, `claude`, `codex`, `cursor` as preset names in these files.
- **AC7 (BLOCK):** The acceptance criteria explicitly requires a "follow-up bead filed for the hard removal of deprecated aliases in a future release." No `.ddx/beads.jsonl` modification or other evidence of bead creation appears in the diff.

post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
